definition: The yield curve's current positive slope is driven primarily by term premium rather than rate expectations, constituting a term premium steepener with fundamentally different growth implications than a genuine mid-cycle expectations steepener. ACM and Kim-Wright decompositions confirm term premium accounts for the majority of the 2s10s positive slope.
evidence: ACM and Kim-Wright decompositions show term premium dominates 2s10s slope
In genuine mid-cycle (2015-2017), slope was driven by rate expectations
Current curve superficially resembles early-to-mid cycle but decomposition differs
pair_3 called this the most operationally valuable insight
Good steepening (productivity-driven, compresses TP) vs bad steepening (fiscal-supply-driven, expands TP) is testable
open_questions: How model-dependent are the ACM vs Kim-Wright decomposition results?
What are the specific positioning implications of a term premium steepener?
Does the Sahm Rule false positive validate this diagnostic methodology?
